speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Why, so. Now have I done a good day’s work.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: You peers, continue this united league.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: I every day expect an embassage

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: From my Redeemer to redeem me hence,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And more in peace my soul shall part to heaven

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Since I have made my friends at peace on Earth.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Rivers and Hastings, take each other’s hand.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Dissemble not your hatred. Swear your love.

speaker: RIVERS
text: By heaven, my soul is purged from grudging hate,

speaker: RIVERS
text: And with my hand I seal my true heart’s love.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: So thrive I as I truly swear the like.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Take heed you dally not before your king,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Lest He that is the supreme King of kings

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Confound your hidden falsehood and award

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Either of you to be the other’s end.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: So prosper I as I swear perfect love.

speaker: RIVERS
text: And I as I love Hastings with my heart.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Madam, yourself is not exempt from this,—

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Nor you, son Dorset,—Buckingham, nor you.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: You have been factious one against the other.—

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Wife, love Lord Hastings. Let him kiss your hand,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And what you do, do it unfeignedly.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: There, Hastings, I will never more remember

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Our former hatred, so thrive I and mine.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Dorset, embrace him.—Hastings, love Lord Marquess.

speaker: DORSET
text: This interchange of love, I here protest,

speaker: DORSET
text: Upon my part shall be inviolable.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: And so swear I.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Now, princely Buckingham, seal thou this league

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: With thy embracements to my wife’s allies

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And make me happy in your unity.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Whenever Buckingham doth turn his hate

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Upon your Grace, but with all duteous love

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Doth cherish you and yours, God punish me

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: With hate in those where I expect most love.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: When I have most need to employ a friend,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And most assurèd that he is a friend,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Deep, hollow, treacherous, and full of guile

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Be he unto me: this do I beg of God,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: When I am cold in love to you or yours.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: A pleasing cordial, princely Buckingham,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Is this thy vow unto my sickly heart.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: There wanteth now our brother Gloucester here

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: To make the blessèd period of this peace.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And in good time

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Here comes Sir Richard Ratcliffe and the Duke.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Good morrow to my sovereign king and queen,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And, princely peers, a happy time of day.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Happy indeed, as we have spent the day.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Gloucester, we have done deeds of charity,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Made peace of enmity, fair love of hate,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Between these swelling, wrong-incensèd peers.

speaker: RICHARD
text: A blessèd labor, my most sovereign lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Among this princely heap, if any here

speaker: RICHARD
text: By false intelligence or wrong surmise

speaker: RICHARD
text: Hold me a foe,

speaker: RICHARD
text: If I unwittingly, or in my rage,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Have aught committed that is hardly borne

speaker: RICHARD
text: By any in this presence, I desire

speaker: RICHARD
text: To reconcile me to his friendly peace.

speaker: RICHARD
text: ’Tis death to me to be at enmity;

speaker: RICHARD
text: I hate it, and desire all good men’s love.

speaker: RICHARD
text: First, madam, I entreat true peace of you,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Which I will purchase with my duteous service;—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Of you, my noble cousin Buckingham,

speaker: RICHARD
text: If ever any grudge were lodged between us;—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Of you and you, Lord Rivers and of Dorset,

speaker: RICHARD
text: That all without desert have frowned on me;—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Of you, Lord Woodeville and Lord Scales;—of you,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Dukes, earls, lords, gentlemen; indeed, of all.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I do not know that Englishman alive

speaker: RICHARD
text: With whom my soul is any jot at odds

speaker: RICHARD
text: More than the infant that is born tonight.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I thank my God for my humility.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: A holy day shall this be kept hereafter.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: I would to God all strifes were well compounded.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: My sovereign lord, I do beseech your Highness

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: To take our brother Clarence to your grace.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Why, madam, have I offered love for this,

speaker: RICHARD
text: To be so flouted in this royal presence?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Who knows not that the gentle duke is dead?

speaker: RICHARD
text: You do him injury to scorn his corse.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Who knows not he is dead! Who knows he is?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: All-seeing heaven, what a world is this!

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Look I so pale, Lord Dorset, as the rest?

speaker: DORSET
text: Ay, my good lord, and no man in the presence

speaker: DORSET
text: But his red color hath forsook his cheeks.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Is Clarence dead? The order was reversed.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But he, poor man, by your first order died,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And that a wingèd Mercury did bear.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Some tardy cripple bare the countermand,

speaker: RICHARD
text: That came too lag to see him burièd.

speaker: RICHARD
text: God grant that some, less noble and less loyal,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Nearer in bloody thoughts, and not in blood,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Deserve not worse than wretched Clarence did,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And yet go current from suspicion.

speaker: STANLEY
text: A boon, my sovereign, for my service done.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: I prithee, peace. My soul is full of sorrow.

speaker: STANLEY
text: I will not rise unless your Highness hear me.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Then say at once what is it thou requests.

speaker: STANLEY
text: The forfeit, sovereign, of my servant’s life,

speaker: STANLEY
text: Who slew today a riotous gentleman

speaker: STANLEY
text: Lately attendant on the Duke of Norfolk.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Have I a tongue to doom my brother’s death,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And shall that tongue give pardon to a slave?

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: My brother killed no man; his fault was thought,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And yet his punishment was bitter death.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Who sued to me for him? Who, in my wrath,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Kneeled at my feet, and bade me be advised?

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Who spoke of brotherhood? Who spoke of love?

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Who told me how the poor soul did forsake

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: The mighty Warwick and did fight for me?

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Who told me, in the field at Tewkesbury,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: When Oxford had me down, he rescued me,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And said “Dear brother, live, and be a king”?

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Who told me, when we both lay in the field

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Frozen almost to death, how he did lap me

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Even in his garments and did give himself,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: All thin and naked, to the numb-cold night?

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: All this from my remembrance brutish wrath

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Sinfully plucked, and not a man of you

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Had so much grace to put it in my mind.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: But when your carters or your waiting vassals

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Have done a drunken slaughter and defaced

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: The precious image of our dear Redeemer,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: You straight are on your knees for pardon, pardon,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And I, unjustly too, must grant it you.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: But for my brother, not a man would speak,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Nor I, ungracious, speak unto myself

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: For him, poor soul. The proudest of you all

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Have been beholding to him in his life,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Yet none of you would once beg for his life.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: O God, I fear Thy justice will take hold

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: On me and you, and mine and yours for this!—

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Come, Hastings, help me to my closet.—

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Ah, poor Clarence.

speaker: RICHARD
text: This is the fruits of rashness. Marked you not

speaker: RICHARD
text: How that the guilty kindred of the Queen

speaker: RICHARD
text: Looked pale when they did hear of Clarence’ death?

speaker: RICHARD
text: O, they did urge it still unto the King.

speaker: RICHARD
text: God will revenge it. Come, lords, will you go

speaker: RICHARD
text: To comfort Edward with our company?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: We wait upon your Grace.
